Container.Name: nws-weather-delta
Container.Overview: Posts a daily Discord recap comparing yesterday's observed NWS weather for two cities (default Des Moines & Providence): high/low temp, humidity, and cloud cover, with color-coded deltas, an optional Claude-generated summary, and a year-to-date scoreboard. Self-scheduling container that fires once per day at POST_TIME (interpreted in City A's timezone). Stdlib-only; the only state is a CSV under /data.
Container.Description: Daily two-city NWS weather comparison posted to a Discord channel via webhook. Color-coded favorability against a seasonal comfort-target curve, optional Claude summary with templated fallback, CSV-backed year-to-date scoreboard.

Config.Name: Discord Webhook URL
Config.Description: Discord webhook the recap is posted to (required).

Config.Name: Post Time (HH:MM)
Config.Description: Local time of day to post, in City A's timezone. Use an early-morning time so both cities have a complete prior day.

Config.Name: Appdata (history.csv)
Config.Description: Where the year-to-date history CSV is stored.

Config.Name: AI Summary Enabled
Config.Description: If true and an API key is set, use a Claude-generated summary; otherwise a templated one.

Config.Name: Anthropic API Key
Config.Description: Optional. Set to enable the AI summary (claude-haiku-4-5 by default). Leave blank for the templated summary.

Config.Name: AI Model
Config.Description: Anthropic model id for the summary.

Config.Name: City A Name
Config.Description: Display name for the first city.

Config.Name: City A Timezone
Config.Description: IANA timezone for City A; also used to interpret POST_TIME.

Config.Name: City B Name
Config.Description: Display name for the second city.

Config.Name: NWS User-Agent
Config.Description: NWS requires a User-Agent with contact info.

Config.Name: Temp Basis
Config.Description: Which temperature is compared to the seasonal target: high or average.
